source: Brighton Collaboration Case Definitions for Myocarditis and Pericarditis
reference: Brighton Collaboration Myocarditis/Pericarditis Working Group. Vaccine. 2004;22(5-6):551-562.
scope: Diagnostic certainty levels for post-vaccination myocarditis and pericarditis in passive surveillance (VAERS).
note: Used in Vax-Beacon Stage 2 (deterministic gatekeeper) and Stage 6 (Brighton L4 early exit guidance).

myocarditis.label: Myocarditis
myocarditis.definition: Inflammation of the myocardium (heart muscle), diagnosed by clinical, laboratory, imaging, or histopathological evidence.

histopathology.label: Histopathological confirmation (EMB)
histopathology.description: Endomyocardial biopsy showing inflammatory infiltrate with myocyte necrosis or damage, per Dallas criteria. Requires multinucleated giant cells for giant cell myocarditis.

cardiac_mri_positive.label: Cardiac MRI positive (Lake Louise criteria)
cardiac_mri_positive.description: Cardiac MRI demonstrating myocardial inflammation per Lake Louise criteria: T2 edema AND late gadolinium enhancement (LGE) in a non-ischemic pattern.

lge_patterns.focal_punctate: Mid-wall or epicardial — consistent with vaccine-associated myocarditis (Nordic Study pattern)
lge_patterns.diffuse: Diffuse mid-wall — suggests viral or systemic etiology (GCM, viral myocarditis)
lge_patterns.subendocardial: Subendocardial — suggests ischemic etiology; requires coronary workup

troponin_elevated.label: Elevated cardiac troponin
troponin_elevated.description: Troponin I or T above the 99th percentile upper reference limit (URL), indicating myocardial injury.

ecg_abnormal.label: ECG abnormalities
ecg_abnormal.description: New electrocardiographic abnormalities consistent with myocarditis: ST-T changes, conduction abnormalities (AV block, bundle branch block), or arrhythmias.
ecg_abnormal.note: Diffuse ST elevation with PR depression suggests pericarditis; focal/territorial ST changes suggest ischemia.

echo_abnormal.label: Echocardiographic abnormalities
echo_abnormal.description: New echocardiographic findings: wall motion abnormalities, reduced ejection fraction (EF), or diastolic dysfunction not explained by prior disease.

compatible_symptoms.label: Compatible clinical symptoms
compatible_symptoms.description: Symptoms consistent with myocarditis: chest pain, dyspnea, palpitations, syncope, or signs of heart failure in the absence of an alternative explanation.

level_1.label: Level 1 — Confirmed
criteria: OR: Cardiac MRI positive (Lake Louise) AND troponin elevated AND compatible symptoms
level_1.clinical_interpretation: Definitive diagnosis. Strongest evidence for pharmacovigilance signal.

level_2.label: Level 2 — Probable
criteria: Troponin elevated AND compatible symptoms AND ≥1 of: (abnormal ECG, abnormal echo, positive MRI)
level_2.clinical_interpretation: High probability. Troponin elevation is required — differentiates from pure pericarditis.

level_3.label: Level 3 — Possible
criteria: Compatible symptoms AND (troponin elevated OR ≥1 abnormal imaging finding)
level_3.clinical_interpretation: Plausible. Incomplete workup or mild presentation.

level_4.label: Level 4 — Insufficient evidence
criteria: Reported as myocarditis but insufficient documented evidence for Level 1–3
level_4.clinical_interpretation: Passive surveillance limitation. Missing diagnostic data — not a clinical exclusion.
level_4.vax_beacon_action: Brighton L4 early exit → Stage 6 directly. Provide structured missing-criteria guidance.
missing_criteria_to_upgrade: troponin_elevated — most common missing criterion in VAERS (order hs-TnI/T)
cardiac_mri_positive — highest upgrade potential (Lake Louise criteria)
ecg_abnormal — often not documented in VAERS reports
echo_abnormal — transthoracic echo frequently absent in VAERS
histopathology — invasive; rarely indicated in stable patients

troponin_missing.rationale: Required for Brighton Level 1–2; supports Level 3. Most commonly missing criterion in VAERS.

tests: Cardiac MRI with T2 mapping and LGE protocol
cardiac_mri_missing.rationale: Enables upgrade to Level 1 (with troponin + symptoms). Differentiates VAM from alternative etiologies by LGE pattern.

ecg_missing.rationale: Supports Level 2 (with troponin + symptoms). Documents conduction abnormalities.

echo_missing.rationale: Supports Level 2 (with troponin + symptoms). Assesses ventricular function and effusion.

pericarditis.label: Pericarditis
pericarditis.definition: Inflammation of the pericardium (fibrous sac surrounding the heart), diagnosed by clinical, laboratory, and imaging criteria.

compatible_symptoms.label: Compatible pericarditis symptoms
compatible_symptoms.description: Chest pain that is sharp, pleuritic (worsened by inspiration), and positional (relieved by sitting forward). Pericardial friction rub on auscultation.

ecg_abnormal.label: ECG changes (pericarditis pattern)
ecg_abnormal.description: New diffuse ST elevation (saddle-shaped, in multiple leads without territorial pattern) and/or PR segment depression. Distinguished from STEMI by diffuse distribution.
ecg_abnormal.note: Diffuse (multi-lead) ST elevation differentiates from ischemic territorial ST elevation.

pericardial_effusion.label: Pericardial effusion
pericardial_effusion.description: Pericardial fluid collection demonstrated by echocardiography or cardiac MRI.

inflammatory_markers_elevated.label: Elevated inflammatory markers
inflammatory_markers_elevated.description: CRP, ESR, or other inflammatory markers elevated above normal range, supporting active pericardial inflammation.

level_1.label: Level 1 — Confirmed
criteria: ≥2 of: (compatible symptoms, ECG changes, pericardial effusion)
level_1.clinical_interpretation: Classic pericarditis presentation with ≥2 objective criteria.

level_2.label: Level 2 — Probable
criteria: ≥1 pericarditis criterion AND elevated inflammatory markers (CRP/ESR)
level_2.clinical_interpretation: Single criterion with laboratory support for inflammation.

level_3.label: Level 3 — Possible
criteria: ≥1 pericarditis criterion without inflammatory marker data
level_3.clinical_interpretation: Plausible but incomplete. Inflammatory marker data could confirm.

level_4.label: Level 4 — Insufficient evidence
criteria: Reported as pericarditis but insufficient documented evidence for Level 1–3
level_4.clinical_interpretation: Passive surveillance limitation. Obtain missing diagnostic data.
level_4.vax_beacon_action: Brighton L4 early exit → Stage 6 directly. Provide structured missing-criteria guidance.
missing_criteria_to_upgrade: compatible_symptoms — chest pain character (pleuritic/positional) often underdocumented
ecg_abnormal — diffuse ST elevation / PR depression pattern
pericardial_effusion — echocardiogram frequently absent in VAERS
inflammatory_markers_elevated — CRP/ESR rarely included in VAERS reports

ecg_missing.rationale: Diffuse ST elevation + PR depression is pathognomonic for pericarditis. Supports Level 1 (with symptoms + effusion).

echo_missing.rationale: Detects pericardial effusion (Brighton criterion). Also excludes myocarditis component.

inflammatory_markers_missing.rationale: Supports Level 2 (with 1 core criterion). Monitors treatment response.

tests: Contact reporter for pleuritic/positional pain history
symptoms_undocumented.rationale: Pleuritic/positional character is a Brighton criterion. Often absent in VAERS free-text.

passive_surveillance_context.description: Brighton Level 4 in VAERS does not indicate the patient does not have myocarditis/pericarditis. It reflects the data limitations of passive surveillance systems where complete diagnostic workups are rarely documented.
passive_surveillance_context.implication: Obtaining missing diagnostic data (medical records, follow-up) can upgrade a Level 4 case to Level 1–3.

myopericarditis.description: Combined myocardial and pericardial involvement. Characterized by pericarditis features (pleuritic chest pain, pericardial effusion, PR depression) plus myocardial injury markers (elevated troponin).
myopericarditis.classification: Apply both myocarditis AND pericarditis criteria; use the higher-certainty level achieved.

vaccine_associated_myocarditis_signal.description: Post-mRNA COVID-19 vaccine myocarditis signal established July 2021 (WHO GACVS). Predominantly young males, after second dose, typically mild and self-limited. LGE pattern: focal/punctate mid-wall (Nordic Study).
vaccine_associated_myocarditis_signal.temporal_window: Most cases within 7 days of vaccination (NAM 2024: 1–7 days, especially 1–4 days post-dose 2)
vaccine_associated_myocarditis_signal.demographic_signal: Young males (12–29 years), dose 2 > dose 1, mRNA vaccines (Pfizer-BioNTech, Moderna)
